_comment: MCP server config template for areas in AI-Skills-German-Law. Copy into <area>/.mcp.json and customize per area. MCP servers extend a skill with live tools: legal databases (Beck-Online, juris, openjur), court calendars, BGBl. lookup, etc. A public MCP server now exists for Bundesrecht and federal case-law via the official NeuRIS open API (see 'rechtsinformationen' below); the top-level .mcp.json at the repo root ships it enabled. No public MCP server exists yet for the commercial databases Beck-Online or juris, so those remain placeholders.

rechtsinformationen._status: WORKING - public MCP server for the official NeuRIS open API (rechtsinformationen.bund.de), run by DigitalService for the Bundesministerium der Justiz. Public beta (Testphase). Queries Bundesrecht plus federal case-law (Bundesgerichte), addressable by ELI/ECLI, returning JSON/XML/HTML. No auth and no Mandatsdaten required, so it is § 203-safe.
rechtsinformationen._install: No published npm package. Clone the repo, run ./quick-setup.sh (npm install && npm run build), then point 'args' below at the built dist/index.js using an absolute path.

_example_beck_online._status: placeholder - no public Beck-Online MCP server exists yet

_example_juris._status: placeholder - no public juris MCP server exists yet

_example_openjur._status: openjur has a public web search; a local MCP wrapper can scrape responsibly

_example_gesetze_im_internet._status: public source, no auth needed; could be a thin wrapper around HTTP. For most Bundesrecht lookups the 'rechtsinformationen' NeuRIS server above is the maintained, official option.

_usage_in_skill: Reference an MCP server from inside a SKILL.md by saying e.g. 'Use the rechtsinformationen MCP server (gesetz_per_eli_abrufen) to fetch the current text of § X' or 'If a beck_online MCP server is available, verify the citation via tool_call.'. The skill remains usable without MCP (model falls back to verification markers).
_note_on_203: An MCP server that ships Mandatsdaten to a remote service is itself a Mandatsgeheimnis-Empfänger and needs the same § 203/§ 43e BRAO contracts as any LLM provider. Self-hosted MCP servers are preferable for client work. The 'rechtsinformationen' NeuRIS server is an exception: it queries only public statutes and case-law and never transmits Mandatsdaten, so it carries no § 203 exposure.
